reference: docs/design/ui-hud-reference/user-references/layout-reference-v2.webp (badge anchor = clean circular crop centered (105,1655) r=98, upscaled 512)
v3_rationale: user judged the v2 badge wrong against the approved reference on device: wide flat red-copper ring with a near-black face ('black hole'). PIL comparison against the reference medallion confirmed: reference is a dark AGED-IRON outer ring with four diamond compass accents, a THIN worn-gold inner accent line, and a textured dark olive-bronze face with a subtle radial sheen (face upper #5A472E / lower #2C2216). v3 regenerates the badge anchored on the exact reference medallion crop; same filename and asset key, content swapped in place. hud-dock-button-cell stays at v2 but is no longer drawn by the dock (the recessed well is token-painted since knife 6).
v2_rationale: user rejected the v1 plates on device: badge read too bright / polished-new (shiny champagne rim, brushed specular face) versus the approved reference's aged matte bronze. v2 regenerates both plates as aged weathered bronze with dark patina, muted worn ring, matte finish; same filenames and asset keys, content swapped in place.

generation.endpoint: images/edits (v3: anchored on the reference medallion circular crop, single element on solid #FF00FF)
generation.sheet: v3: single 1024x1024 badge on solid #FF00FF; gen-1 accepted (face tone medians #48412A/#332C1C vs reference #5A472E/#2C2216 - in family)

steps: chroma key magenta inline PIL (r>100 && b>100 && r-g>60 && b-g>60 -> alpha 0)
opaque bbox crop +2px pad, square canvas, scale to 256*0.985, center paste
defringe: a>0 && r>g+40 && b>g+40 -> clamp r<=g+45, b<=g+25

states.active: code overlay: champagne ring/border highlight + label brightening (no extra generated art)
states.disabled: code globalAlpha dim (no extra generated art)

intended_use: bottom dock chrome: hud-dock-badge-round for the capital/tasks edge badges, hud-dock-button-cell for the recessed-strip center cells; drawn sizes now derive from UiThemeTokens.getDockMetrics(width) canvas-width ratios
